name: KrillApp.Trigger.LowThreshold
title: Low Threshold
description: Triggers an alarm when the observed DataPoint's value falls at or below a configured threshold. The threshold is stored in this trigger's own meta.snapshot.value as a stringified number (TriggerMetaData has no value field). Compares the DataPoint snapshot value against the threshold: fires when value <= threshold, and nodes observing this trigger as a source are invoked. Sets node state to WARN on breach.
shortDescription: Triggers when a DataPoint value falls at or below a configured lower limit.
llmPurpose: Evaluates whether the observed DataPoint's value is at or below a threshold; on breach it raises an alarm state and fires, invoking its observers.
llmBehavior: invoked when the observed DataPoint updates (SOURCE_INVOKED)
reads the observed DataPoint's snapshot value
compares value <= threshold from its own meta.snapshot.value
sets node state to WARN on breach
on breach, fires; nodes observing this trigger are invoked
llmInputs: threshold stored in this trigger's meta.snapshot.value (stringified number)
llmOutputs: observers invoked when threshold is breached
llmCreationHints: Create a LowThreshold when the user wants to be alerted or trigger actions when a value falls below a minimum. Store the threshold as a stringified number in the trigger's meta.snapshot.value, and wire the DataPoint into the trigger's sources (SOURCE_INVOKED) and inputs. Actions observe this trigger via their own sources.

llmConnectionHints.role: conditional trigger that fires (invoking its observers) when the observed DataPoint value is at or below threshold
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the DataPoint in sources (SOURCE_INVOKED) and inputs; the threshold lives in meta.snapshot.value; actions list this trigger in their own sources.

llmExamples: Alert me when the temperature drops below 32 degrees.
Trigger a webhook when the water level is below 10.
Warn me when battery drops to 20%.
